proven.claim: Proven: the walk-up loop always terminates and the function always returns a string.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 160
; Proven: the walk-up loop always terminates and the function always returns a string.
; Every tree-sitter AST is finite; each iteration advances to .parent; null terminates.

(declare-const ast_depth           Int)  ; depth from node to tree root
(declare-const loop_iterations     Int)  ; iterations before current === null
(declare-const function_returns    Int)  ; 1 = function returns a string value

; Code transition: tree-sitter guarantees a finite acyclic parent-pointer chain
(assert (>  ast_depth 0))
(assert (<= ast_depth 100000))

; Code transition: each iteration sets current = current.parent (moves one level up)
(assert (>= loop_iterations 0))
(assert (<= loop_iterations ast_depth))

; Code transition: if the loop finds no match, line ~184 returns "<anonymous>"
; (non-empty, always defined)
(assert (= function_returns 1))

; Negation: function does NOT return a string
(assert (= function_returns 0))
(check-sat)

violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 160
; Violation — scope attribution error:
; When the callback-argument block (lines 141–158) falls through (e.g., via the P6 path above),
; the walk-up loop at line 160 starts from node.parent, which is still the "arguments" node.
; Traversing upward: arguments → call_expression → [variable_declarator for the outer call].
; The walk-up finds the variable_declarator of the OUTER CALL's assignment
; (e.g., `const result = obj.method(callback)`) and returns "result" as the callback's name.
; This name belongs to the outer call's container, not to the callback function itself.
; No existing principle covers this: it is not a lifecycle, state machine, information flow,
; or idempotency issue — it is a scope attribution failure during AST walk-up.

(declare-const callback_block_fell_through  Int)  ; 1 = lines 141–158 returned nothing
(declare-const walksup_starts_at_arguments  Int)  ; 1 = walk-up begins at "arguments" node
(declare-const outer_call_assigned_to_var   Int)  ; 1 = outer call is RHS of variable_declarator
(declare-const walksup_finds_outer_var_decl Int)  ; 1 = walk-up reaches outer variable_declarator
(declare-const returned_name_is_outer_var   Int)  ; 1 = returned name is outer variable (e.g. "result")
(declare-const name_describes_callback      Int)  ; 1 = name meaningfully identifies the callback fn

; Code transition: callback-argument block fell through (either P6 path or undefined callFn)
(assert (= callback_block_fell_through 1))

; Code transition: walk-up at line 161 initializes current = node.parent = "arguments" node
(assert (= walksup_starts_at_arguments callback_block_fell_through))

; Code transition: the outer call is assigned to a variable:
;   const result = obj.method(callback)
; This means: arguments → call_expression → variable_declarator (with name field "result")
(assert (= outer_call_assigned_to_var 1))
(assert (= walksup_finds_outer_var_decl outer_call_assigned_to_var))

; Code transition: walk-up loop at line 162 checks current.type === "variable_declarator"
; and fires: return current.childForFieldName("name").text  → returns "result"
(assert (= returned_name_is_outer_var walksup_finds_outer_var_decl))

; Code transition: the returned name belongs to the outer call's assignment context,
; not to the callback function being named
(assert (= name_describes_callback (- 1 returned_name_is_outer_var)))

; Violation: callback function is labeled with the outer variable's name
(assert (= returned_name_is_outer_var  1))
(assert (= name_describes_callback     0))
(check-sat)

; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 160
; Proven: the walk-up loop always terminates and the function always returns a string.
; Every tree-sitter AST is finite; each iteration advances to .parent; null terminates.

(declare-const ast_depth           Int)  ; depth from node to tree root
(declare-const loop_iterations     Int)  ; iterations before current === null
(declare-const function_returns    Int)  ; 1 = function returns a string value

(assert (<= ast_depth 100000))

(assert (<= loop_iterations ast_depth))

; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 160
; Violation — scope attribution error:
; When the callback-argument block (lines 141–158) falls through (e.g., via the P6 path above),
; the walk-up loop at line 160 starts from node.parent, which is still the "arguments" node.
; Traversing upward: arguments → call_expression → [variable_declarator for the outer call].
; The walk-up finds the variable_declarator of the OUTER CALL's assignment
; (e.g., `const result = obj.method(callback)`) and returns "result" as the callback's name.
; This name belongs to the outer call's container, not to the callback function itself.
; No existing principle covers this: it is not a lifecycle, state machine, information flow,
; or idempotency issue — it is a scope attribution failure during AST walk-up.

(declare-const callback_block_fell_through  Int)  ; 1 = lines 141–158 returned nothing
(declare-const walksup_starts_at_arguments  Int)  ; 1 = walk-up begins at "arguments" node
(declare-const outer_call_assigned_to_var   Int)  ; 1 = outer call is RHS of variable_declarator
(declare-const walksup_finds_outer_var_decl Int)  ; 1 = walk-up reaches outer variable_declarator
(declare-const returned_name_is_outer_var   Int)  ; 1 = returned name is outer variable (e.g. "result")
(declare-const name_describes_callback      Int)  ; 1 = name meaningfully identifies the callback fn

; Code transition: callback-argument block fell through (either P6 path or undefined callFn)
(assert (= callback_block_fell_through 1))

; Code transition: walk-up at line 161 initializes current = node.parent = "arguments" node
(assert (= walksup_starts_at_arguments callback_block_fell_through))

; Code transition: the outer call is assigned to a variable:
;   const result = obj.method(callback)
; This means: arguments → call_expression → variable_declarator (with name field "result")
(assert (= outer_call_assigned_to_var 1))
(assert (= walksup_finds_outer_var_decl outer_call_assigned_to_var))

; Code transition: walk-up loop at line 162 checks current.type === "variable_declarator"
; and fires: return current.childForFieldName("name").text  → returns "result"
(assert (= returned_name_is_outer_var walksup_finds_outer_var_decl))

; Code transition: the returned name belongs to the outer call's assignment context,
; not to the callback function being named
(assert (= name_describes_callback (- 1 returned_name_is_outer_var)))

; Violation: callback function is labeled with the outer variable's name
(assert (= returned_name_is_outer_var  1))
(assert (= name_describes_callback     0))
(check-sat)
